function.name: web_search
function.description: Search the public internet for information. Each result will contain a url, a title, and one excerpt taken directly from the page.

start_date.description: (optional) The start date for the search range in the format YYYY-MM-DD. Must not be equal to end_date.

end_date.description: (optional) The end date for the search range in the format YYYY-MM-DD. If the value is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

number_of_results.description: (optional) The number of search results to return.

function.name: retrieve_information
function.description: Retrieves critical data segments from a previously ingested document via hybrid lexical and semantic calculations. Crucial Requirement: The search prompt argument MUST contain the token placeholder containing your storage key inside double curly brackets, exactly format formatted as: {{key_name}} alongside your query description text.

prompt.description: Your target question containing the explicit target reference mapping (e.g., 'What were the total research costs in {{my_document_key}}?').

preserve_document_order.description: Set to True if processing complex structural data dependencies across sequential disclosures.

function.name: parse_html_page
function.description: Parses an HTML document or SEC filing. It automatically extracts key metadata, converts asset tables to clean markdown, slices sections into optimal word chunks, and runs concurrent contextual LLM enrichment before updating the disk cache.

url.description: The target URL of the HTML page/filing to process.

key.description: The unique cache storage key for saving and loading the index.

function.name: edgar_search
function.description: Search the EDGAR Database through the SEC API. You should provide a search query. You can also optionally provide a start date, an end date, a page number, top N results, a list of form types, and/or a list of CIKs. The results are returned as a list of dictionaries, each containing the metadata for a filing. It does not contain the full text of the filing.

search_query.description: The case-insensitive search-term or phrase to search the contents of filings and their attachments. This can be a single word, phrase, or combination of words and phrases. Supported search features include wildcards (*), Boolean operators (OR, NOT), and exact phrase matching by enclosing phrases in quotation marks ("exact phrase"). By default, all terms are joined by an implicit AND operator.

form_types.description: (optional) Limits search to specific EDGAR form types (e.g., ['8-K', '10-Q']) list of strings. Default: all form types

ciks.description: (optional) Filters results to filings from specified CIKs, type list of strings. Leading zeros are optional but may be included. Example: [ "0001811414", "1318605" ]. Default: all CIKs

start_date.description: (optional) Start date for the search range in yyyy-mm-dd format. If the value is a date that is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

end_date.description: (optional) End date for the search range, in the same format as startDate. If the value is a date that is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

page.description: (optional) Used for pagination. Each page contains up to 100 matching filings. Increase the page number to retrieve the next set of 100 filings. Example: 3 retrieves the third page. Default: 1

top_n_results.description: (optional) Return only the first N results out of 100 from the page. If not provided, all 100 results will be returned. E.g. if page is 2, and top_n_results is 10, you will receive results 100 to 110.

function.name: calculator
function.description: Evaluate a mathematical expression and return the result. Use this tool for all arithmetic calculations instead of computing by hand. Supports: +, -, *, /, ** (exponentiation), % (modulo), and parentheses for grouping. Available functions: abs(), min(), max(), sqrt(), log(), log10(). Examples: '(5000 - 3200) * 0.21', '(2865507 / 1905871) ** 0.5 - 1', '14060 / 2148'.

function.name: price_history
function.description: Fetch historical daily price data for a specific asset class. Returns a CSV table with one row per day. Use asset_class='equity' or 'etf' for US-listed stocks/ETFs (e.g. AAPL, SPY), 'crypto' for pairs like btcusd (lowercase, no dash), or 'fx' for pairs like audusd. Non-US equities and most indices/futures are not covered by the pricing provider. Each row includes raw OHLC (open/high/low/close), split- and dividend-adjusted OHLC (adjOpen/adjHigh/adjLow/adjClose), raw and adjusted volume, divCash (dividend amount on the date), and splitFactor.

ticker.description: Ticker symbol. High-level symbology rules: (1) Equity/ETF/mutual fund: use the US exchange-listed symbol (AAPL, SPY, VFIAX). Symbols are case-insensitive and contain only letters and dashes. (2) Share classes use a dash, not a dot: 'BRK-A' / 'BRK-B' (not 'BRK.A'), 'BF-B'. (3) Securities are identified by the ticker as listed on the primary US exchange; non-US listings, OTC pink sheets, indices, and futures are generally not covered. (4) Tickers can be reused after delisting; data is point-in-time for the entity that held the ticker on each date. (5) Crypto: concatenated lowercase pair with no separator, e.g. 'btcusd', 'ethusd'. (6) FX: concatenated lowercase pair with no separator, e.g. 'audusd', 'eurusd'.

asset_class.description: Asset class used to route to the proper pricing endpoint.

function.name: submit_final_result
function.description: Submits the final answer to the user. You should include your final answer, as well as any necessary reasoning, justification, calculations, and explanation. Finally, you should provide any sources used to answer the question. You MUST use this tool to submit your final result. The user will not see your response if you do not use this tool to submit. You will not be able to continue working after this tool is called; the conversation will be ended.

final_result.description: The final result to submit to the user

steps.message:
You are an IPO and financial diligence agent.

You are given a question and must answer it using the tools provided.

You cannot interact with the user or ask clarifying questions.

Answer solely based on available information and tools.

Assume the current date is May 31, 2026.

---

TOOLS AND DATA SOURCES

You have access to a document storage system.

You may store parsed HTML retrieved from the web and later retrieve
information using LLM-based retrieval prompts.

Use this system to avoid context-window limitations.

When working with long filings, prefer section-specific retrieval prompts
rather than broad document-level retrieval.

Examples:

- retrieve the Segment Adjusted EBITDA reconciliation table from MD&A
- retrieve the glossary definition of Starshield
- retrieve the controlled-company governance disclosure

For peer-comparison questions:

- retrieve peer SEC filings using edgar_search
- do not rely on memory for peer financial figures

For historical public-equity price data not available in SEC filings:

- use price_history as primary source
- use raw unadjusted prices unless adjusted prices are requested
- fall back to web_search only if price_history fails

Stock indices are not covered by price_history.

Use authoritative sources such as FRED or web_search for index levels.

---

SOURCE HIERARCHY

Prioritize sources in this order:

1. S-1 / S-1A filings
2. Incorporated SEC filings
3. SEC exhibits and merger documents
4. SEC periodic filings
5. Company earnings materials
6. External sources

If figures differ across sources:

prefer the most authoritative SEC source containing the directly reported
figure and explain material discrepancies when relevant.

SEC filings are the primary and most authoritative source of financial and
disclosure information.

---

ANSWER STANDARDS

You must call submit_final_result with the completed answer.

Your submission will not be processed unless submit_final_result is called.

Include:

- complete reasoning
- calculations
- supporting analysis

You will be evaluated on:

- final-answer accuracy
- analytical correctness

Do not round intermediate calculations.

Round only final reported values.

When possible, report calculated figures to at least two decimal places.

Report financial figures using the same scale and units used in the filing
unless otherwise specified.

Share prices should be reported with two decimal places.

---

DISCLOSURE DISCIPLINE

Before reaching a conclusion:

determine whether the filing provides sufficient information to answer:

- directly
- partially
- only through inference

Distinguish carefully among:

- facts explicitly disclosed
- reasonable filing-supported inferences
- information not disclosed

Do not speculate beyond what the filing supports.

If material information is absent:

explicitly state that the filing does not provide sufficient information to
conclude.

Absence of disclosure may itself be analytically material.

---

FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENTS

Treat separately:

- historical facts
- roadmap targets
- commercialization expectations
- management intentions
- forward-looking statements

Do not present aspirations or plans as historical outcomes.

---

GOVERNANCE AND LEGAL QUESTIONS

For governance, legal, or disclosure-language questions:

use the filing's precise terminology whenever possible.

Distinguish among:

- requirements
- exemptions
- company elections
- forward-looking language

---

ACCOUNTING POLICY QUESTIONS

For accounting-policy questions:

1. Identify the relevant disclosed accounting policy or treatment
2. Determine whether the filing explicitly resolves the issue
3. If unresolved, state that the filing does not specify treatment

---

NON-GAAP QUESTIONS

For non-GAAP measures:

identify:

- the starting GAAP metric
- each disclosed adjustment
- whether economically recurring costs are excluded

Do not accept non-GAAP measures without analyzing their construction.

---

AUDIENCE TAGS

Questions may include audience tags such as:

[PUBLIC | BANK]
[VC | CREDIT]
[LEGAL | ACCOUNTING]

Use these tags to calibrate terminology and analytical emphasis while
remaining grounded in disclosed facts.

---

SOURCES

At the end of your answer provide:

{
  "sources": [
    {
      "url": "...",
      "name": "..."
    }
  ]
}


---


FILING CONTEXT

The primary filing for this benchmark is the SpaceX S-1 filed May 20, 2026:

https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1181412/000162828026036936/spaceexplorationtechnologi.htm

Important:

The consolidated financial statements have been retrospectively recast under
common-control accounting to include:

- xAI (merged February 2, 2026)
- X Holdings (merged into xAI March 28, 2025)

for all historical periods presented.

No period reflects standalone SpaceX operations exclusively.

When interpreting historical segment figures, state whether they reflect the
recast consolidated entity.

SpaceX (SPCX) has no public trading history as of May 30, 2026.

Do not attempt price_history retrieval for SpaceX or SPCX.

For peer valuation or peer financial benchmarking, retrieve peer-company SEC
filings using edgar_search rather than relying on memory.

Question:
Following the xAI and X Holdings mergers, what related-party relationships exist within the consolidated structure, and where does the filing discuss pre-merger intercompany activity?

steps.reasoning_content:
The user is asking about related-party relationships within the consolidated SpaceX structure following the xAI and X Holdings mergers, and where the filing discusses pre-merger intercompany activity. I need to parse the SpaceX S-1 filing and then retrieve information about related-party relationships and intercompany activity.

Let me start by parsing the filing.

results.content: SUCCESS: Loaded portable assets for key: spacex_s1

steps.reasoning_content: Good, the filing has been parsed. Now let me retrieve information about related-party relationships and intercompany activity.

arguments.prompt: What related-party relationships exist within the consolidated structure following the xAI and X Holdings mergers? Retrieve the related-party transactions disclosure from {{spacex_s1}}
Where does the filing discuss pre-merger intercompany activity between SpaceX, xAI, and X Holdings? Retrieve intercompany transactions and eliminations from {{spacex_s1}}

results.content:
Based on the provided prospectus preamble and financial statement notes, the acquisitions of xAI and X Holdings were accounted for as **reorganizations of entities under common control** because Elon Musk held a controlling financial and majority voting interest in SpaceX, xAI, and X prior to the mergers. As a result, all historical intercompany transactions between SpaceX, xAI, and X were eliminated in consolidation. 

However, the newly consolidated structure maintains extensive, ongoing related-party relationships and transactions with external entities affiliated with Elon Musk, SpaceX board members, and underwriters. 

Here is the retrieved disclosure of related-party relationships and transactions within the consolidated structure:

### 1. Elon Musk and Affiliated Entities
Elon Musk serves as the Founder, CEO, CTO, and Chairman of SpaceX, while simultaneously holding leadership and ownership roles in several other ventures that transact with the consolidated company.

*   **Tesla, Inc.** (Musk is CEO, Technoking, director, and ~20% shareholder; Tesla owns <1.0% of SpaceX Class A stock):
    *   **Equipment & Vehicle Purchases:** The company purchased massive amounts of energy and vehicle products from Tesla, including Megapack products ($191M in 2024; $506M in 2025; $34M in Q1 2026) and Cybertrucks ($131M in 2025), recorded in Property, Plant, and Equipment.
    *   **Commercial, Licensing & Support Agreements:** SpaceX obtained goods/services from Tesla ($11M in 2023; $4M in 2024; $144M in 2025). xAI also obtained goods/services ($191M in 2024; $506M in 2025) and recognized minor revenue from Tesla.
    *   **Advertising:** Tesla purchased advertising on the X platform ($0.5M in 2024; $4M in 2025).
    *   **Aircraft Usage:** SpaceX invoiced Tesla for the use of SpaceX-owned aircraft by Musk and Tesla personnel ($1M in 2023; $1M in 2024; $2M in 2025).
    *   **Strategic Investment:** In January 2026, Tesla agreed to invest **$2,000 million** in xAI Series E Preferred Stock. Following the xAI Merger, this right converted into SpaceX Class A Common Stock, resulting in the issuance of **3.8 million shares** to Tesla in March 2026.
*   **The Boring Company** (Infrastructure company founded by Musk):
    *   **Leases & Construction:** X leases office space from a Boring Company subsidiary ($0.1M in 2024; $1M in 2025). Additionally, SpaceX incurred $1M in 2025 for the construction of tunnels in Bastrop, Texas.
*   **Musk Industries LLC** (Owned by Musk):
    *   **Real Estate Lease:** xAI leases real property from this entity ($0.5M in 2024; $2M in 2025; $0.2M in early 2026).
*   **Musk-Owned Security Company:**
    *   **Security Services:** SpaceX pays a security company organized to protect Musk, including during his SpaceX duties ($2M in 2023; $3M in 2024; $4M in 2025; $1M in early 2026).
*   **Elon Musk Trust:**
    *   **Share Repurchases:** In 2025, Musk's trust purchased **$1,421 million** of common stock from current and former employees.

### 2. Antonio J. Gracias and Valor Equity Partners
Antonio J. Gracias is a member of the SpaceX Board of Directors and the founder, CEO, and CIO of Valor Equity Partners ("Valor").
*   **Massive AI Infrastructure Leases (Failed Sale-Leasebacks):** xAI subsidiaries entered into three major equipment lease agreements with Valor for AI computing hardware. The aggregate cash payments over the life of these leases are staggering: **$6,986 million**, **$6,633 million**, and **$6,587 million** (guaranteed by SpaceX). 
    *   *Accounting Treatment:* These were deemed "failed sale-leaseback transactions." As of March 31, 2026, the company recorded **$1,121 million** in current debt and **$7,920 million** in non-current debt on the consolidated balance sheet related to these Valor agreements, alongside $186 million in interest expense for Q1 2026.
*   **X API Services:** X received payments from Valor for API services ($1M in 2024; $1M in 2025).

### 3. Other Directors and Executive Officers
*   **Corporate Aircraft:** Through its subsidiary Falcon Landing, LLC, SpaceX owns and operates three aircraft specifically for use by its directors and executive officers (specific financial amounts for this bullet are cut off in the source text, but the relationship is formally disclosed).

### 4. Underwriters and Financial Affiliates
The prospectus discloses potential conflicts of interest regarding the financial institutions underwriting the offering, as they are deeply embedded in the company's financial and advisory structure:
*   **Advisory Roles:** Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC advised SpaceX in connection with the acquisition of xAI.
*   **Lending & Credit Facilities:** Affiliates of the underwriters (including Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, BofA, Citigroup, J.P. Morgan, Barclays, Deutsche Bank, RBC, UBS, and Wells Fargo) serve as lenders, administrative agents, or co-agents under the **SpaceX Bridge Loan** and the **SpaceX Credit Facility**. 

*(Note: Because the xAI and X mergers were common-control reorganizations, any historical transactions that occurred strictly between SpaceX, xAI, and X prior to the mergers—such as internal funding or shared services—have been entirely eliminated in the consolidated financial statements and are not presented as external related-party transactions.)*

Based on the provided text excerpts, the filing **does not disclose specific details, amounts, or business descriptions of pre-merger intercompany activity** strictly between SpaceX, xAI, and X Holdings. 

However, it does address the **elimination** of their intercompany activities in the context of accounting for the mergers. Here is exactly what the text provides regarding this topic:

### 1. Intercompany Eliminations (Accounting Treatment)
In the sections explaining the retrospective recasting of the financial statements and Note 1 (Nature of Business), the filing states that because the mergers were between entities under common control (Elon Musk), they were accounted for as reorganizations. The text explicitly notes the elimination of their internal transactions:
> *"The Company’s consolidated financial statements have been prepared to reflect the retrospective combination of the net assets of the entities at their historical carrying amounts for all periods presented. No new goodwill or other intangible assets have been recorded and **all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation**."* 
*(Note: This exact paragraph appears twice in the provided text, once in the Preamble summary and once in Note 1).*

### 2. Clarification on "Related Person Transactions"
While the text contains a detailed section titled **"CERTAIN RELATIONSHIPS AND RELATED PERSON TRANSACTIONS,"** these do *not* describe intercompany activity between SpaceX, xAI, and X. Instead, they detail transactions between the SpaceX/xAI/X entities and Elon Musk's **other affiliated, external entities**, including:
*   **Tesla:** Commercial, licensing, support, and advertising agreements, as well as aircraft usage and Tesla's $2B investment in xAI.
*   **The Boring Company:** Office space leases for X and tunnel construction expenses for SpaceX.
*   **Musk Industries LLC:** Real property leases for xAI.
*   **Musk's Security Company:** Security services incurred by SpaceX.

**Summary:** 
If you are looking for the specific financial amounts or operational details of what SpaceX, xAI, and X bought from or sold to *each other* prior to the mergers, that information is **not present** in the provided text fragments. The filing only confirms that whatever those historical internal transactions were, they have been entirely **eliminated in consolidation** to prepare the retrospective combined financial statements.

steps.reasoning_content: I have good information now. Let me also retrieve more specific details about the common-control accounting treatment and where exactly in the filing these discussions appear.

arguments.prompt: Retrieve the exact section headings and locations where the filing discusses common-control accounting, retrospective recasting, and elimination of intercompany transactions between SpaceX, xAI, and X Holdings from {{spacex_s1}}
Retrieve the Note 1 Nature of Business section describing the xAI merger and X Holdings merger, including the dates and accounting treatment as common-control reorganizations from {{spacex_s1}}

results.content:
Based on the provided text, the discussions regarding common-control accounting, retrospective recasting, and the elimination of intercompany/related-party transactions between SpaceX, xAI, and X Holdings occur in the following exact section headings and locations. 

*(Note: While the structural tags label all provided text as `[SECTION: PREAMBLE]`, the text itself contains distinct internal document headings and sections where these accounting treatments are detailed.)*

### 1. Prospectus Preamble / Summary Historical Financial Data
**Location:** Early preamble sections introducing forward-looking statement disclaimers, corporate definitions, and the summary historical financial data.
* **Retrospective Recasting & Common-Control Accounting:** 
  > *"The consolidated financial statements of SpaceX have been retrospectively recast for all periods presented to include (i) the historical results of X.AI Holdings Corp., which was acquired by SpaceX, effective February 2, 2026 (the “xAI Merger”), and X Holdings Corp. (“X Holdings”), which was acquired by xAI, effective March 28, 2025 (the “X Merger”), because these transactions were between entities under common control..."*
* **Summary Data Recasting:**
  > *"The summary historical consolidated financial and operating data of SpaceX has been prepared to reflect the retrospective combination of the companies for all periods presented to include the historical results of xAI... and X Holdings... because these transactions were between entities under common control."*
* **MD&A / Forward-Looking Statements Context:**
  > *"Our audited consolidated financial statements and related notes have been prepared to reflect the retrospective combination of the companies for all periods presented as the acquisitions of xAI and X Holdings were accounted for as transactions between entities under common control."*

### 2. NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS (Note 1 / Nature of Business)
**Location:** Following the introduction of the company's three operating segments (Space, Connectivity, and AI) and the timeline of the Mergers.
* **Common-Control Accounting, Retrospective Combination & Elimination of Transactions:**
  > *"The Mergers have been accounted for as reorganizations of entities under common control as Mr. Elon Musk had a controlling financial interest in the Company, xAI and X through his majority voting interest in each such entity during the periods presented in these consolidated financial statements. The Company’s consolidated financial statements have been prepared to reflect the retrospective combination of the net assets of the entities at their historical carrying amounts for all periods presented. No new goodwill or other intangible assets have been recorded and all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation."*
* **Change in Reporting Entity:**
  > *"The capital stock and shareholders’ equity for all periods presented reflects a continuation of the historical SpaceX capital stock and shareholders’ equity, combined with the historical capital stock and shareholders’ equity of X and xAI merged under common control... This presentation constitutes a change in reporting entity."*

### 3. Note 2 - Summary of Significant Accounting Policies
**Location:** Immediately following the merger accounting explanations and the justification for omitting separate financial statements for xAI and X.
* **Elimination of Intercompany Transactions:**
  > *"The consolidated financial statements include the accounts of the Company and its wholly owned subsidiaries. All intercompany balances and transactions have been eliminated in consolidation."*

### 4. Related Party Transactions (Elon Musk & Affiliated Entities)
**Location:** The section detailing commercial, licensing, and support agreements between the consolidated entities (SpaceX, xAI, X) and Tesla/The Boring Company. 
* **Context for Eliminations:** While this section outlines the *gross* historical related-party transactions with external affiliated entities (like Tesla), it provides the underlying context for the internal eliminations mentioned in Note 1, noting that transactions strictly *between* the merging entities (SpaceX, xAI, and X) were eliminated upon consolidation due to the common-control reorganization.

On February 2, 2026 (“xAI Merger Date”), the Company completed its acquisition of X.AI Holdings Corp. (“xAI”), pursuant to which xAI became a wholly-owned subsidiary of the Company (“xAI Merger”). Prior to the xAI Merger, on March 28, 2025, xAI completed its acquisition of X Holdings Corp. (“X”) and X.AI Corp., in which X and X.AI Corp. became wholly-owned subsidiaries of xAI (“X Merger”, and collectively with xAI Merger, “Mergers”). X.AI Corp began operations in March 2023 and Twitter, Inc. (“Twitter”) was acquired by Mr. Elon Musk in October 2022. The Mergers were each effected through a share exchange.

The Mergers have been accounted for as reorganizations of entities under common control as Mr. Elon Musk had a controlling financial interest in the Company, xAI and X through his majority voting interest in each such entity during the periods presented in these consolidated financial statements. The Company’s consolidated financial statements have been prepared to reflect the retrospective combination of the net assets of the entities at their historical carrying amounts for all periods presented. No new goodwill or other intangible assets have been recorded and all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation. The capital stock and shareholders’ equity for all periods presented reflects a continuation of the historical SpaceX capital stock and shareholders’ equity, combined with the historical capital stock and shareholders’ equity of X and xAI merged under common control, as adjusted by the respective exchange ratios used to effect the Mergers, except for xAI’s historical redeemable convertible preferred stock through the date of the xAI Merger. All of xAI’s redeemable convertible preferred stock were converted to SpaceX common stock as part of the xAI Merger and are presented as such from the date of the xAI Merger. This presentation constitutes a change in reporting entity. Refer to Note 12 - Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Shareholders’ Equity for additional details.

steps.reasoning_content: Now I have comprehensive information. Let me also check for any additional related-party relationships, particularly around the Valor Equity Partners leases and the underwriter relationships.

arguments.prompt: Retrieve the Valor Equity Partners lease agreements, failed sale-leaseback transactions, and the aggregate payment obligations from {{spacex_s1}}
Retrieve the section on certain relationships and related person transactions including the underwriter conflicts of interest and board member relationships from {{spacex_s1}}

results.content:
Based on the provided text, here are the details regarding the Valor Equity Partners lease agreements, failed sale-leaseback transactions, and aggregate payment obligations:

### **1. Lease Agreements**
Certain subsidiaries of xAI (including CTC) have entered into equipment lease, sublease, and access agreements with Valor Equity Partners ("Valor"), an entity affiliated with board member Antonio J. Gracias. These include:
*   **Three Computing Equipment Leases:** Agreements where an xAI subsidiary leases computing and related equipment from Valor. The lessees' payments and performance obligations under these agreements are guaranteed by Space Exploration Technologies Corp. (SpaceX) or one of its subsidiaries.
*   **"Valor transaction" (October 2025):** An equipment lease agreement entered into by CTC (amended on November 10, 2025) for certain AI infrastructure hardware.
*   **"Valor transaction II" (January 2026):** A subsequent equipment lease agreement entered into by CTC (amended on February 18, 2026) for additional AI infrastructure hardware. 

### **2. Failed Sale-Leaseback Transactions**
The AI infrastructure hardware leases (Valor transaction and Valor transaction II) were deemed **failed sale-leaseback transactions** for accounting purposes, resulting in the company recording them as debt rather than standard operating/finance leases:
*   **As of December 31, 2025:** Following the initial Valor transaction, the Company recorded related debt of **$455 million** (current) and **$4,052 million** (non-current) on the consolidated balance sheets, alongside $66 million in Interest expense for the year.
*   **As of March 31, 2026:** Following both the November 2025 and January 2026 transactions, the combined related debt for these failed sale-leaseback transactions grew to **$1,121 million** (current) and **$7,920 million** (non-current). The Company also recorded $186 million in Interest expense for the three months ended March 31, 2026, related to these equipment lease agreements.

### **3. Aggregate Payment Obligations**
The three primary computing equipment lease agreements carry massive aggregate cash payment obligations over the life of the leases:
*   **First equipment lease:** **$6,986 million**
*   **Second equipment lease:** **$6,633 million**
*   **Third equipment lease:** **$6,587 million**

*Note on Payments Made:* Pursuant to these lease agreements, the subsidiaries had already made cash payments of **$885 million in 2025**, and **$857 million** from January 1, 2026, through February 28, 2026. Additionally, X received minor API service payments from Valor ($1 million in 2024, $1 million in 2025, and $0.1 million in early 2026).

Based on the provided S-1 filing excerpts, here is the detailed extraction of the sections concerning **Certain Relationships and Related Person Transactions**, **Board Member Relationships**, and **Underwriter Conflicts of Interest**.

### 1. Certain Relationships and Related Person Transactions
The company has established a written policy requiring the audit committee to review and approve any "related person transaction" exceeding **$120,000** involving directors, executive officers, >5% shareholders, or their immediate family members. Following the February 2026 xAI Merger, this section also encompasses ongoing agreements originally entered into by xAI or X Holdings.

**Transactions with Elon Musk and Affiliated Entities:**
*   **Tesla, Inc.:** 
    *   Tesla beneficially owns 18,990,195 shares of Class A common stock (less than 1.0% post-offering).
    *   SpaceX maintains commercial, licensing, and support agreements with Tesla on arm's-length terms. 
    *   As of December 31, 2025, the Company purchased **$506 million** in Megapack products and **$131 million** in Cybertrucks at MSRP from Tesla for use in property, plant, and equipment.
*   **The Boring Company:** 
    *   X entered into an office space lease with a Boring Company subsidiary (payments of $0.1M in 2024, $1M in 2025, and $0.1M in early 2026). 
    *   SpaceX incurred **$1 million** in 2025 for tunnel construction by The Boring Company in Bastrop, Texas.
*   **Musk Industries LLC:** xAI leases real property from this Musk-owned entity ($0.5M in 2024, $2M in 2025, $0.2M in early 2026).
*   **Security Services:** SpaceX utilizes a Musk-owned security company for personal and corporate security services, incurring expenses of $2M (2023), $3M (2024), $4M (2025), and $1M (early 2026).

### 2. Board Member Relationships & Director Conflicts
The filing highlights specific financial ties and potential competitive conflicts involving members of the board of directors:

*   **Antonio J. Gracias & Valor Equity Partners:** 
    *   Antonio J. Gracias, a company director, is the Founder, CEO, and CIO of Valor Equity Partners.
    *   Certain subsidiaries (including CTC/xAI) entered into AI infrastructure hardware equipment lease agreements with Valor. 
    *   These were accounted for as **failed sale-leaseback transactions**. As of March 31, 2026, the Company recorded **$1,121 million** in current debt, **$7,920 million** in non-current debt, and **$186 million** in interest expenses related to these leases.
*   **Donald Harrison:** The board noted that Harrison is employed by an organization that does business with the Company, though the transaction amounts fell below the materiality thresholds (greater of $200,000 or 5% of revenue).
*   **Competitive Conflicts & Corporate Opportunities:** Directors and key employees may serve on the boards of or have financial relationships with competing space, AI, or satellite communications ventures. Furthermore, the company's charter explicitly **renounces certain corporate opportunities** presented to Elon Musk and specific directors, meaning they have no duty to present these outside opportunities to the Company.

### 3. Underwriter Conflicts of Interest
The underwriting syndicate comprises full-service financial institutions with deep, pre-existing financial and advisory ties to the Company and its affiliates, creating potential conflicts of interest:

*   **Advisory Roles:** **Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC** specifically advised the Company in connection with the acquisition of xAI.
*   **Lending & Credit Facilities:** Affiliates of the underwriters are heavily entrenched in the Company's debt structure. 
    *   *SpaceX Bridge Loan:* Affiliates of Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, BofA, Citigroup, J.P. Morgan, Barclays, Deutsche Bank, RBC, UBS, and Wells Fargo serve as lenders or administrative agents.
    *   *SpaceX Credit Facility:* Affiliates of BofA, Citigroup, Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, Barclays, Deutsche Bank, Wells Fargo, RBC, and UBS serve as lenders.
*   **Directed Share Program (DSP):** At the Company's request, underwriters reserved a percentage of the IPO shares for sale to employees, management, business partners, and **friends and family of management**. Shares purchased through the DSP are **exempt from standard lock-up restrictions**, meaning these insiders can sell immediately, reducing the public float and presenting a structural conflict of interest regarding IPO allocation.

### 4. Governance & Control Context
*   **Controlled Company Status:** Post-IPO, Elon Musk will hold majority voting power via Class B common stock. The Company will rely on "controlled company" exemptions under Nasdaq rules, bypassing certain independent board and committee requirements.
*   **Investors' Rights Agreement:** Certain existing shareholders (covered under the Related Person Transactions section) hold contractual rights to demand registration of their shares, allowing them to bypass Rule 144 holding periods and potentially flood the market with stock post-lock-up.

arguments.final_result:
## Related-Party Relationships Within the Consolidated SpaceX Structure (Post-Merger)

Following the xAI Merger (February 2, 2026) and the X Merger (March 28, 2025), the S-1 identifies a web of related-party relationships spanning Elon Musk's affiliated entities, board members, and underwriting institutions. These are detailed primarily in the section titled **"Certain Relationships and Related Person Transactions"** and in **Note 1 (Nature of Business)** and **Note 2 (Summary of Significant Accounting Policies)** of the consolidated financial statements.

---

### 1. Elon Musk and Affiliated External Entities

Elon Musk serves as Founder, CEO, CTO, and Chairman of SpaceX while simultaneously holding leadership and ownership roles in several other ventures that transact with the consolidated entity:

| Related Party | Relationship | Key Transactions |
|---|---|---|
| **Tesla, Inc.** | Musk is CEO, Technoking, director, ~20% shareholder; Tesla owns <1.0% of SpaceX Class A stock | Megapack purchases ($191M in 2024; $506M in 2025; $34M in Q1 2026); Cybertruck purchases ($131M in 2025); commercial/licensing/support services ($11M in 2023; $4M in 2024; $144M in 2025 from SpaceX; $191M in 2024; $506M in 2025 from xAI); Tesla advertising on X ($0.5M in 2024; $4M in 2025); aircraft usage invoiced to Tesla ($1M in 2023; $1M in 2024; $2M in 2025); Tesla's $2,000M investment in xAI Series E (converted to 3.8M shares of SpaceX Class A in March 2026) |
| **The Boring Company** | Infrastructure company founded by Musk | X leases office space from a Boring Company subsidiary ($0.1M in 2024; $1M in 2025; $0.1M in early 2026); SpaceX incurred $1M in 2025 for tunnel construction in Bastrop, Texas |
| **Musk Industries LLC** | Owned by Musk | xAI leases real property ($0.5M in 2024; $2M in 2025; $0.2M in early 2026) |
| **Musk-Owned Security Company** | Organized to protect Musk | SpaceX pays for security services including during SpaceX duties ($2M in 2023; $3M in 2024; $4M in 2025; $1M in early 2026) |
| **Elon Musk Trust** | Controlled by Musk | Purchased $1,421M of common stock from current and former employees in 2025 |

### 2. Antonio J. Gracias and Valor Equity Partners

Antonio J. Gracias is a SpaceX board member and the Founder, CEO, and CIO of Valor Equity Partners. This relationship generates the single largest related-party financial exposure in the filing:

- **AI Infrastructure Equipment Leases (Failed Sale-Leasebacks):** xAI subsidiaries (including CTC) entered into three major computing equipment lease agreements with Valor, guaranteed by SpaceX. Aggregate cash payment obligations over the life of the leases total:
  - First lease: **$6,986 million**
  - Second lease: **$6,633 million**
  - Third lease: **$6,587 million**
- These were accounted for as **failed sale-leaseback transactions**, recorded as debt rather than operating leases. As of March 31, 2026: **$1,121 million** current debt and **$7,920 million** non-current debt on the consolidated balance sheet, with **$186 million** in interest expense for Q1 2026 alone.
- Cash payments already made: $885M in 2025 and $857M from January 1 through February 28, 2026.
- **X API Services:** X received minor payments from Valor for API services ($1M in 2024; $1M in 2025; $0.1M in early 2026).

### 3. Other Directors and Executive Officers

- **Donald Harrison:** Employed by an organization that does business with the Company, though transaction amounts fell below the materiality threshold (greater of $200,000 or 5% of revenue).
- **Corporate Aircraft:** Through subsidiary Falcon Landing, LLC, SpaceX owns and operates three aircraft for use by directors and executive officers.
- **Competitive Conflicts:** The charter explicitly renounces certain corporate opportunities presented to Musk and specific directors, meaning they have no duty to present outside opportunities to the Company.

### 4. Underwriters and Financial Affiliates

- **Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC** advised SpaceX on the xAI acquisition.
- Affiliates of Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, BofA, Citigroup, J.P. Morgan, Barclays, Deutsche Bank, RBC, UBS, and Wells Fargo serve as lenders or agents under both the **SpaceX Bridge Loan** and the **SpaceX Credit Facility**.
- A **Directed Share Program** reserves IPO shares for employees, management, business partners, and friends/family of management, exempt from standard lock-up restrictions.

---

## Pre-Merger Intercompany Activity: Where the Filing Discusses It

The filing **does not disclose specific details, amounts, or business descriptions of pre-merger intercompany transactions** between SpaceX, xAI, and X Holdings. Instead, it addresses their elimination in the following locations:

### Location 1: Prospectus Preamble / Summary Historical Financial Data
The preamble states:
> *"The consolidated financial statements of SpaceX have been retrospectively recast for all periods presented to include (i) the historical results of X.AI Holdings Corp., which was acquired by SpaceX, effective February 2, 2026 (the 'xAI Merger'), and X Holdings Corp. ('X Holdings'), which was acquired by xAI, effective March 28, 2025 (the 'X Merger'), because these transactions were between entities under common control..."*

### Location 2: Note 1 — Nature of Business (Consolidated Financial Statements)
This is the most explicit discussion. The filing states:
> *"The Mergers have been accounted for as reorganizations of entities under common control as Mr. Elon Musk had a controlling financial interest in the Company, xAI and X through his majority voting interest in each such entity during the periods presented in these consolidated financial statements. The Company's consolidated financial statements have been prepared to reflect the retrospective combination of the net assets of the entities at their historical carrying amounts for all periods presented. No new goodwill or other intangible assets have been recorded and **all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation**."*

The note further clarifies that this presentation constitutes a **change in reporting entity**, with capital stock and shareholders' equity for all periods reflecting a continuation of historical SpaceX equity combined with X and xAI equity, adjusted by the respective exchange ratios.

### Location 3: Note 2 — Summary of Significant Accounting Policies
The standard consolidation policy states:
> *"The consolidated financial statements include the accounts of the Company and its wholly owned subsidiaries. All intercompany balances and transactions have been eliminated in consolidation."*

### Key Analytical Takeaway

Because the mergers were common-control reorganizations (not arm's-length acquisitions), **all pre-merger transactions between SpaceX, xAI, and X Holdings were eliminated** in the retrospectively recast financial statements. The filing does not break out or quantify what those internal transactions were — only that they have been fully eliminated. Consequently, no period in the financial statements reflects standalone SpaceX operations; every historical period presents the combined entity as if the three companies had always been consolidated. This is analytically material because it means investors cannot isolate SpaceX's pre-merger standalone financial performance from the S-1.

---

  "sources": [
    {
      "url": "https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1181412/000162828026036936/spaceexplorationtechnologi.htm",
      "name": "SpaceX S-1 Registration Statement (Filed May 20, 2026)"
    }
  ]
}
